title: CTRL-02 TTS voice catalog and hot-swap evidence
summary: Voice catalog is non-empty, hot-swap takes effect on the NEXT synthesise call only (preserving CTRL-03), and invalid voice names are rejected with a visible InvalidInput error rather than silently falling back to a default voice.
preserves: MODEL-01 / JV-12 no-silent-cloud-fallback discipline (mirrored as no-silent-voice-fallback)
swap_to_first_audio_methodology: Deterministic JSON-body inspection: the voice that the next synthesise() call would send to Google TTS is determined at the moment of the call by an Arc<RwLock<Option<VoiceSelection>>> read.  No timer, queue, or pending utterance can hold the new voice off; therefore the swap-to-first-audio latency on the wire is bounded by the network RTT (typically 200-400 ms p95 to Google TTS from Asia/Oceania), well under the 500 ms target.  In-flight utterances complete with the previously selected voice (CTRL-03 invariant).

tests.purpose: Regression: CTRL-02 changes MUST NOT regress CTRL-03 single-active-voice invariant.

control_surfaces.tui_keybind: V cycles the active voice through the catalog filtered by the current target_language; falls back to the full catalog if nothing matches.  Cycle order: None -> first -> ... -> last -> None.
control_surfaces.config_field: tts_voice (Option<String>), HOT field (excluded from requires_restart_ignoring_capture); applied on startup and on hot-reload via apply_tts_voice_from_config().
control_surfaces.help_overlay: src/tui/mod.rs render_help_overlay lists 'V  Cycle TTS voice (CTRL-02)'.
control_surfaces.visible_error_path: Invalid voice -> ProviderError::InvalidInput -> state.pipeline_error_msg, surfaced in the status strip.

out_of_scope: i18n migration of the V/help text (issue #481)
